text: Science has a clearer story about mirages than social media does. Ever seen water on a hot road? That’s a mirage, but it’s not what it seems. Mirages happen due to light bending through layers of air with different temperatures. That’s called refraction! It tricks our brains into seeing things that aren’t really there. So when you think you see water, it’s actually an illusion shaped by the heat. Curious about other amazing science facts? Hit that follow button for more!
